input: What is the company's vacation policy?
actual_output: Full-time employees accrue 15 days of paid vacation per year, increasing to 20 days after 5 years of service.
retrieval_context: Section 4.1 Vacation: Full-time employees accrue paid vacation at a rate of 15 days per calendar year. After five (5) years of continuous service, the accrual rate increases to 20 days per year. Vacation may be carried over up to a maximum of 30 days.

input: What is the parental leave policy?
actual_output: Primary caregivers receive 16 weeks of paid parental leave; secondary caregivers receive 4 weeks.
retrieval_context: Section 5.2 Parental Leave: The company provides sixteen (16) weeks of fully paid parental leave for primary caregivers and four (4) weeks of fully paid parental leave for secondary caregivers, applicable to birth, adoption, or foster placement.

input: What is the performance review cycle?
actual_output: Performance reviews are conducted annually in December, with mid-year check-ins in June.
retrieval_context: Section 8.1 Performance Management: The company conducts formal annual performance reviews in December of each year. Mid-year check-in conversations are held in June to assess progress against goals and provide developmental feedback.

input: What is the 401(k) matching policy?
actual_output: The company matches 100% of employee contributions up to 4% of salary.
retrieval_context: Section 6.3 Retirement Benefits: The company provides a 401(k) plan with employer matching of 100% of employee contributions up to four percent (4%) of eligible compensation. Employees are eligible to participate after 90 days of employment.

input: What is the remote work policy?
actual_output: Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week with manager approval.
retrieval_context: Section 3.4 Remote Work: Eligible employees may work remotely up to three (3) days per week, subject to manager approval and business needs. Employees must be available during core hours of 10 AM to 3 PM local time.

input: What is the code of conduct policy on conflicts of interest?
actual_output: Employees must disclose any potential conflicts of interest to HR and their manager.
retrieval_context: Section 2.3 Conflicts of Interest: Employees must promptly disclose any actual or potential conflict of interest to their manager and Human Resources. This includes outside employment, financial interests in competitors, and personal relationships with vendors.

input: What is the anti-harassment policy?
actual_output: The company has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment and discrimination of any kind.
retrieval_context: Section 2.1 Anti-Harassment: The company maintains a zero-tolerance policy for harassment, discrimination, and retaliation. This policy applies to all employees, contractors, and visitors. Violations may result in immediate termination.

input: What is the health insurance benefit?
actual_output: The company covers 80% of health insurance premiums for employees and 60% for dependents.
retrieval_context: Section 6.1 Health Insurance: The company provides medical, dental, and vision insurance. The company pays 80% of employee premiums and 60% of dependent premiums. Employees may enroll during the annual open enrollment period or within 30 days of a qualifying life event.

input: What is the probationary period for new employees?
actual_output: New employees have a 90-day probationary period during which employment may be terminated without notice.
retrieval_context: Section 1.3 Probationary Period: All new employees are subject to a ninety (90) day introductory period. During this period, employment may be terminated at any time without prior notice. Completion of the introductory period does not guarantee continued employment.

input: What is the expense reimbursement policy?
actual_output: Business expenses must be submitted within 30 days with receipts for amounts over $25.
retrieval_context: Section 7.1 Expense Reimbursement: Employees will be reimbursed for reasonable and necessary business expenses. Expense reports must be submitted within thirty (30) days of incurring the expense. Receipts are required for all expenses over $25.

input: What is the sick leave policy?
actual_output: Employees receive 10 days of paid sick leave per year, which does not carry over.
retrieval_context: Section 4.2 Sick Leave: Full-time employees receive ten (10) days of paid sick leave per calendar year. Sick leave does not carry over to the following year and has no cash value upon termination.

input: What is the tuition reimbursement benefit?
actual_output: The company reimburses up to $5,250 per year for job-related education expenses.
retrieval_context: Section 6.5 Tuition Reimbursement: The company reimburses up to $5,250 per calendar year for tuition, fees, and books for job-related courses at accredited institutions. Employees must receive a grade of B or better and remain employed for one year after course completion.

input: What is the disciplinary process?
actual_output: The disciplinary process includes verbal warning, written warning, final written warning, and termination.
retrieval_context: Section 9.1 Progressive Discipline: The company generally follows a progressive discipline process: (1) verbal warning, (2) written warning, (3) final written warning with performance improvement plan, (4) termination. Serious violations may result in immediate termination.

input: What is the overtime policy?
actual_output: Non-exempt employees receive 1.5x their regular rate for hours worked over 40 per week.
retrieval_context: Section 3.2 Overtime: Non-exempt employees will be paid overtime at one and one-half (1.5) times their regular rate of pay for all hours worked in excess of forty (40) hours in a workweek. Overtime must be pre-approved by a manager.

input: What is the bereavement leave policy?
actual_output: Employees receive up to 5 days of paid bereavement leave for immediate family members.
retrieval_context: Section 4.4 Bereavement Leave: Employees may take up to five (5) days of paid bereavement leave for the death of an immediate family member (spouse, child, parent, sibling). Up to three (3) days are provided for extended family members.

input: What is the drug and alcohol policy?
actual_output: The company maintains a drug-free workplace and prohibits the use of alcohol or drugs during work hours.
retrieval_context: Section 2.5 Drug-Free Workplace: The company maintains a drug-free workplace. Employees are prohibited from using, possessing, or being under the influence of alcohol or illegal drugs during work hours, on company premises, or while operating company vehicles.

input: What is the social media policy?
actual_output: Employees must not share confidential company information on social media and should identify personal opinions as their own.
retrieval_context: Section 2.7 Social Media: Employees must not share confidential or proprietary company information on social media. When discussing company-related topics, employees must clearly identify their statements as personal opinions and not official company positions.

input: What is the whistleblower protection policy?
actual_output: Employees who report suspected violations in good faith are protected from retaliation.
retrieval_context: Section 2.9 Whistleblower Protection: The company prohibits retaliation against any employee who reports suspected violations of law, regulation, or company policy in good faith. Employees may report concerns anonymously through the ethics hotline.

input: What is the non-compete agreement requirement?
actual_output: Employees in certain roles must sign a non-compete agreement restricting work for competitors for 12 months after leaving.
retrieval_context: Section 10.2 Non-Competition: Employees in designated roles (engineering, sales, and executive) are required to sign a non-competition agreement. The agreement restricts employment with direct competitors for twelve (12) months following separation.

input: What is the company's equal opportunity employment policy?
actual_output: The company is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on any protected characteristic.
retrieval_context: Section 1.1 Equal Opportunity: The company is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring, promotion, compensation, or any other employment decision based on race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.

input: What is the jury duty leave policy?
actual_output: Employees receive paid leave for jury duty for up to 10 days per year.
retrieval_context: Section 4.6 Jury Duty: Employees summoned for jury duty will receive their regular pay for up to ten (10) days per calendar year. Employees must provide a copy of the jury summons to HR and return to work on days when not required for jury service.

input: What is the employee referral bonus?
actual_output: Employees receive a $2,000 bonus for referring a candidate who is hired and completes 90 days of employment.
retrieval_context: Section 6.7 Employee Referral Program: Employees who refer a candidate who is subsequently hired and completes ninety (90) days of employment will receive a referral bonus of $2,000. The bonus is paid in the next regular payroll cycle after the 90-day milestone.

input: What is the company's policy on personal device use?
actual_output: Personal devices used for work must be enrolled in the mobile device management system.
retrieval_context: Section 11.3 BYOD Policy: Employees who use personal devices to access company systems or data must enroll their device in the company's mobile device management (MDM) system. The company may remotely wipe company data from enrolled devices.

input: What is the severance pay policy?
actual_output: Eligible employees receive one week of severance pay per year of service, up to a maximum of 26 weeks.
retrieval_context: Section 9.4 Severance: Employees involuntarily terminated without cause may be eligible for severance pay of one (1) week per year of completed service, up to a maximum of twenty-six (26) weeks, subject to signing a separation agreement.

input: What is the flexible spending account benefit?
actual_output: Employees may contribute up to $2,850 per year to a healthcare FSA.
retrieval_context: Section 6.2 Flexible Spending Accounts: Employees may contribute up to $2,850 per plan year to a Healthcare Flexible Spending Account (FSA) and up to $5,000 per household to a Dependent Care FSA. Contributions are made on a pre-tax basis.

input: What is the policy on workplace violence?
actual_output: The company has zero tolerance for workplace violence and threats of violence.
retrieval_context: Section 2.4 Workplace Violence: The company has zero tolerance for workplace violence, threats of violence, or intimidating behavior. Employees who experience or witness such behavior must report it immediately to HR or security.

input: What is the background check policy for new hires?
actual_output: All job offers are contingent on successful completion of a background check including criminal history and employment verification.
retrieval_context: Section 1.4 Background Checks: All offers of employment are contingent upon successful completion of a background check, which may include criminal history, employment verification, education verification, and reference checks.

input: What is the company's policy on gifts and entertainment?
actual_output: Employees may not accept gifts valued over $50 from vendors or business partners.
retrieval_context: Section 2.6 Gifts and Entertainment: Employees may not accept gifts, meals, or entertainment valued at more than $50 from any vendor, customer, or business partner. All gifts received must be reported to HR. Gifts of cash or cash equivalents are never acceptable.

input: What is the paid holidays schedule?
actual_output: The company observes 11 paid holidays per year including New Year's Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Thanksgiving, and Christmas.
retrieval_context: Section 4.3 Holidays: The company observes eleven (11) paid holidays per year: New Year's Day, Martin Luther King Jr. Day, Presidents Day, Memorial Day, Independence Day, Labor Day, Columbus Day, Veterans Day, Thanksgiving Day, the day after Thanksgiving, and Christmas Day.

input: What is the employee assistance program?
actual_output: The company provides a free EAP offering confidential counseling and support services.
retrieval_context: Section 6.6 Employee Assistance Program: The company provides all employees and their household members with access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP). The EAP offers up to eight (8) free counseling sessions per issue per year, plus referrals for legal, financial, and other support services.

input: What is the policy on intellectual property created by employees?
actual_output: All work product created by employees in the scope of their employment belongs to the company.
retrieval_context: Section 10.1 Work Product: All inventions, discoveries, works of authorship, and other intellectual property created by employees within the scope of their employment or using company resources are the exclusive property of the company.

input: What is the company's lactation accommodation policy?
actual_output: The company provides reasonable break time and a private space for nursing mothers.
retrieval_context: Section 5.4 Lactation Accommodation: The company provides reasonable unpaid break time for nursing mothers to express breast milk for up to one year after the child's birth. A clean, private space (not a bathroom) is available for this purpose.

input: What is the military leave policy?
actual_output: Employees on military leave receive differential pay to make up the difference between military and regular pay for up to 12 months.
retrieval_context: Section 4.7 Military Leave: Employees called to active military duty will receive differential pay (the difference between military pay and regular base salary) for up to twelve (12) months. Benefits continue during military leave as required by USERRA.

input: What is the policy on workplace accommodations for disabilities?
actual_output: The company provides reasonable accommodations for employees with disabilities through an interactive process.
retrieval_context: Section 1.5 Disability Accommodation: The company provides reasonable accommodations to qualified employees with disabilities. Employees requesting accommodations should contact HR to initiate the interactive process. Medical documentation may be required.

input: What is the company's policy on political activities?
actual_output: Employees may not engage in political activities on company time or use company resources for political purposes.
retrieval_context: Section 2.8 Political Activities: Employees may not engage in political activities during work hours or use company resources (equipment, facilities, or funds) for political purposes. The company does not reimburse employees for political contributions.

input: What is the stock option vesting schedule?
actual_output: Stock options vest over 4 years with a 1-year cliff, then monthly thereafter.
retrieval_context: Section 6.8 Equity Compensation: Stock options and RSUs granted to employees vest over a four (4) year period with a one (1) year cliff. After the cliff, vesting occurs monthly on a pro-rata basis. Vesting accelerates upon a change of control.

input: What is the policy on personal relationships at work?
actual_output: Employees in direct reporting relationships must disclose romantic relationships to HR.
retrieval_context: Section 2.10 Workplace Relationships: Employees who enter into a romantic relationship with a direct report or supervisor must disclose the relationship to HR. The company may require a transfer to eliminate the reporting relationship.

input: What is the company's smoking policy?
actual_output: Smoking is prohibited in all company facilities and within 25 feet of building entrances.
retrieval_context: Section 2.11 Smoke-Free Workplace: Smoking, including e-cigarettes and vaping, is prohibited in all company facilities, vehicles, and within twenty-five (25) feet of any building entrance or air intake.

input: What is the policy on use of company equipment?
actual_output: Company equipment is for business use only and employees have no expectation of privacy when using it.
retrieval_context: Section 11.1 Company Equipment: Company-owned equipment, including computers, phones, and vehicles, is provided for business use. Employees have no expectation of privacy when using company equipment. The company reserves the right to monitor usage.

input: What is the pay frequency?
actual_output: Employees are paid bi-weekly on Fridays.
retrieval_context: Section 3.1 Pay Schedule: Employees are paid on a bi-weekly basis, every other Friday. The pay period runs from Sunday through Saturday. Direct deposit is available and strongly encouraged.

input: What is the company's policy on moonlighting?
actual_output: Employees must disclose outside employment that could conflict with their duties or the company's interests.
retrieval_context: Section 2.3 Outside Employment: Employees must disclose any outside employment or consulting activities to HR. Outside employment that creates a conflict of interest, competes with the company, or interferes with job performance is prohibited.

input: What is the employee stock purchase plan?
actual_output: Employees may purchase company stock at a 15% discount through payroll deductions.
retrieval_context: Section 6.9 Employee Stock Purchase Plan: Eligible employees may participate in the Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP), which allows the purchase of company stock at a 15% discount to the lower of the market price at the beginning or end of each six-month offering period.

input: What is the policy on confidential information?
actual_output: Employees must protect confidential company information and may not disclose it to unauthorized parties.
retrieval_context: Section 10.3 Confidentiality: Employees must protect the confidentiality of company information, including trade secrets, customer data, financial information, and business strategies. This obligation continues after employment ends.

input: What is the company's policy on workplace safety?
actual_output: Employees must follow all safety procedures and report unsafe conditions immediately.
retrieval_context: Section 12.1 Workplace Safety: The company is committed to providing a safe work environment. Employees must follow all safety procedures, use required protective equipment, and immediately report unsafe conditions, accidents, or near-misses to their manager or HR.

input: What is the policy on attendance and punctuality?
actual_output: Employees are expected to be at work on time and must notify their manager at least one hour before their shift if they will be absent.
retrieval_context: Section 3.3 Attendance: Regular attendance and punctuality are essential. Employees who will be absent or late must notify their manager at least one (1) hour before their scheduled start time. Excessive absenteeism may result in disciplinary action.

input: What is the company's dress code policy?
actual_output: The company has a business casual dress code, with formal attire required for client meetings.
retrieval_context: Section 3.5 Dress Code: The company maintains a business casual dress code. Employees are expected to dress professionally and appropriately for their role. Business formal attire is required when meeting with clients or attending formal company events.

input: What is the policy on company vehicles?
actual_output: Employees authorized to use company vehicles must have a valid driver's license and follow all traffic laws.
retrieval_context: Section 11.4 Company Vehicles: Employees authorized to operate company vehicles must maintain a valid driver's license and clean driving record. Employees must comply with all traffic laws and may not use company vehicles for personal use without prior approval.

input: What is the short-term disability benefit?
actual_output: Short-term disability provides 60% of base salary for up to 12 weeks after a 7-day waiting period.
retrieval_context: Section 6.4 Short-Term Disability: The company provides short-term disability insurance that pays sixty percent (60%) of base salary for up to twelve (12) weeks, following a seven (7) day elimination period. The benefit coordinates with state disability programs.

input: What is the long-term disability benefit?
actual_output: Long-term disability provides 60% of base salary after 90 days of disability, up to age 65.
retrieval_context: Section 6.4 Long-Term Disability: Long-term disability insurance provides sixty percent (60%) of base salary after a ninety (90) day elimination period, continuing until recovery, age 65, or death, whichever occurs first.

input: What is the life insurance benefit?
actual_output: The company provides basic life insurance equal to 2x annual salary at no cost to employees.
retrieval_context: Section 6.4 Life Insurance: The company provides basic life insurance coverage equal to two times (2x) the employee's annual base salary at no cost to the employee. Employees may purchase additional voluntary life insurance for themselves and dependents.
